methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $40M total raised proxy.

private.name: Shinkei
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Daylight Computer
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Whisper Aero is a Series A consumer tech company founded by Mark Moore and Ian Villa that has raised $40M+. The valuation assessment draws on a limited comp universe of two private peers—Shinkei and Daylight Computer—with no public comparables available in the sector.

Stage-progression analysis, the primary applicable method here, uses the $40M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage transitions. This approach yields an implied valuation of $80M. Given the sparse methodology landscape and limited comp visibility, a triangulated range of $68M (low) to $92M (high) frames the analysis, with $80M as the median point.

The analysis carries material uncertainty. With only one quantitative method and two private comps available, there is insufficient data density to validate the 2.0x multiplier or assess whether Whisper Aero's fundamentals justify positioning within that range. The absence of public market comparables restricts benchmarking to peer-relative growth and profitability metrics. A fuller valuation would require visibility into revenue run-rate, unit economics, or explicit cash-flow projections. The current range should be treated as preliminary pending access to additional comp multiples or operational performance data.
